context: Agent must split a fat interface into role-specific interfaces aligned to actual client usage, without over-granularizing into single-method interfaces

checklist.name: Read-only interface produced with correct methods
checklist.description: A read-only interface file exists containing findById, findAll, and findByFilter — no write or admin methods

checklist.name: Write interface produced with correct methods
checklist.description: A writable interface file exists containing at minimum save and delete (and may extend or include read methods)

checklist.name: Admin interface produced with correct methods
checklist.description: An admin interface file exists containing truncate, exportDump, and importDump — no read or write methods

checklist.name: No single-method interfaces created
checklist.description: No produced interface contains only one method when the client that uses it always calls multiple methods from the same role group

checklist.name: isp-analysis.md identifies the ISP violation correctly
checklist.description: isp-analysis.md names at least one client (e.g., ReportingService or AdminMigrationTool) that was forced to depend on methods it never uses

checklist.name: isp-analysis.md explains the resolution
checklist.description: isp-analysis.md explains that splitting by client role means each client only depends on the interface relevant to its own usage
